name: Peña Labra y Pico Tres Mares – trail run desde el collado de Fuente del Chivo
region_intro: Jogging around Polaciones offers routes through a rugged mountainous terrain in western Cantabria, characterized by significant elevation changes and extensive forests. The region features prominent peaks of the Cantabrian Mountains and deep valleys carved by fast-flowing rivers. Runners can explore diverse landscapes, from challenging ascents to more accessible paths within a well-preserved natural environment. Best jogging routes around Polaciones The most popular jogging route is Nacimiento del río Pisuerga por la Senda de Fuente Cobre (PR-P-8) — circular trailrunning por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Montaña Palentina Natural Park. Another top favourite among local runners is Peña Labra y Pico Tres Mares – trail run desde el collado de Fuente del Chivo, a difficult 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail offers views of Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares from Fuente del Chivo. Local runners also love the Trans-Cantabrian Trail, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Jogging around Polaciones is defined by rugged mountainous terrain, extensive beech and oak forests, and deep river valleys. The network of running routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Polaciones are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Polaciones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes in Polaciones?
A: Polaciones offers a diverse and challenging landscape for runners. You'll encounter rugged mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes, extensive beech and oak forests providing shaded paths, and deep valleys carved by fast-flowing rivers. This mix of gradients, natural paths, and varied surfaces is ideal for gradient training and running on mixed terrain.

Q: Are there many running routes available in Polaciones?
A: Yes, komoot features around 10 dedicated running routes in Polaciones. These routes cater to various preferences, though most are categorized as difficult due to the region's mountainous nature. You'll find options ranging from moderate paths to more challenging trails.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while jogging in Polaciones?
A: Polaciones is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see prominent peaks of the Cantabrian Mountains like Peña Sagra, Pico Tres Mares, and Peña Labra. The region is also part of the Saja National Reserve, offering pristine natural environments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas, and the La Cohilla reservoir greets visitors at the entrance to Polaciones, enhancing the scenic experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Polaciones?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120143103/nacimiento-del-rio-pisuerga-por-la-senda-de-fuente-cobre-pr-p-8-circular-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Pisuerga por la Senda de Fuente Cobre (PR-P-8)&lt;/a&gt; is a circular trailrunning route exploring the Montaña Palentina Natural Park.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of running trails in Polaciones?
A: Most running trails in Polaciones are considered &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;. The region's mountainous character means routes often involve significant elevation gain, with some trails featuring over 1000 meters of ascent. While there is one moderate route, the majority offer a challenging experience suitable for experienced runners.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Polaciones?
A: The running routes in Polaciones are highly regarded by the komoot community. Over 80 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the immersive experience in nature, the dramatic mountain scenery, and the opportunity to jog through ancient oak forests and quaint villages.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trail runners seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak – Tres Mares loop from Estación de Esquí y Montaña Alto Campoo&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding route spanning over 18 kilometers with more than 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering an enduring and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views of the mountains?
A: Yes, many routes in Polaciones provide spectacular panoramic views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999275589/pena-labra-y-pico-tres-mares-trail-run-desde-el-collado-de-fuente-del-chivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra y Pico Tres Mares – trail run desde el collado de Fuente del Chivo&lt;/a&gt; offers incredible vistas of Peña Labra and Pico Tres Mares.

Q: What kind of natural environment will I experience while running in Polaciones?
A: You'll be immersed in a well-preserved natural environment. Polaciones boasts lush forests of beech and oak trees, contributing to its rich biodiversity. As part of the Saja National Reserve, the area is committed to conservation, ensuring a wild and unspoiled setting for your runs, with diverse ecosystems and potential wildlife sightings.

Q: Are there any routes that explore specific natural parks?
A: Yes, some routes venture into significant natural areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120143103/nacimiento-del-rio-pisuerga-por-la-senda-de-fuente-cobre-pr-p-8-circular-trailrunning-por-el-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Pisuerga por la Senda de Fuente Cobre (PR-P-8)&lt;/a&gt;, for example, explores the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, offering varied terrain and spectacular views within a protected landscape.

Q: What is the highest point I might reach on a running route in Polaciones?
A: Polaciones is characterized by significant elevation, with some peaks exceeding 2,000 meters. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak – Tres Mares loop&lt;/a&gt; can take you to high altitudes, offering challenging ascents and breathtaking views from prominent summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Very close to the Alto Campo ski resort and located at 2,149 meters above sea level, the top of Tres Mares is one of those peaks that you cannot miss. You will have wonderful views towards the Cantabrian interior, the Picos de Europa National Park and the neighboring Montaña Palentina Natural Park in the Castilla y León region.

At 2029 meters above sea level, the top of Peña Labra is located in a privileged place. It represents the border between the Community of Cantabria and Castilla y León, so to the north you will have impressive views of the Cantabrian interior and the mountains of the Picos de Europa National Park, while to the south you will be able to appreciate another wonderful landscape such as the of the Montaña Palencia Natural Park.
